violations.text: Shellstock located in the walk-in cooler did not have the proper identification tags. Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the shellstock are received until the container is empty. Tag was placed with shellstock during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

Roasted garlic in oil was observed held at 72 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items were voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

Working containers (spray bottles) used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Spray bottles were marked with chemical names during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on the 1-door reach-in. Refrigerator door gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2025

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of walk-in cooler #1 and the ice machine have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025
The door to both establishments toilet room is not self-closing. Toilet room doors shall be self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2025
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/25/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. -Staff washed, rinsed, and sanitized the can opener during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/25/2023

Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair in the 1 door Arctic Air refrigerator and bottom unit of the cold drawer. Gaskets shall be repaired or replaced. *This violation has not exceed the original correction date of 09/01/23. New Violation. Correct By: 09/01/2023

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (salt used to sprinkle on salad ) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. -Staff voluntarily disposed of the salad during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer) were observed with food debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Staff washed, rinsed, and sanitized the can opener and slicer during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023

The bottom unit of salad station observed held at 47 degrees Fahrenheit. The bottom shelves of 1 door Arctic refrigerator observed at 45-47 degrees F. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. -No TCS foods observed in the bottom unit of salad station. Cut tomatoes observed at 50 degrees F. on bottom shelf in the Arctic-Air refrigerator. Per interview of staff, tomatoes were cut 1 hour prior to this inspection. Cut tomatoes were moved to a different cold hold unit during this inspection. -Bulky containers observed stored on top shelves/racks in the 1 door Arctic-Air refrigerator and may prevent proper cold air flow circulating in unit. -Service was contacted. Establishment shall not store TCS foods in the salad station and 1 door Arctic-Air refrigerator until both units are able to maintain an CONSISTENT ambient of 41 degrees F. or below. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023

ROP salmon observed in reduced oxygen environment and observed completely thawed out in the cold drawer. ROP salmon observed at 45 degrees F. Proper thawing of ROP seafood products reviewed. -Staff voluntarily disposed of the ROP salmon during this inspection. -ROP salmon observed at 37 degrees F. and observed in reduced oxygen environment in the walk-in cooler. -Staff removed the salmon from reduced oxygen packaging during this inspection. *Labels on salmon says, "Keep Frozen". Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/18/2023

Bottom unit of the cold drawer observed in poor repair in the kitchen area. Equipment components such as doors, seals, hinges, fasteners, and kick plates shall be kept intact, tight, and adjusted in accordance with manufacturer's specifications. New Violation. Correct By: 09/01/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine observed with black residue. The non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent buildup of residue. New Violation. Correct By: 08/25/2023
